name: Turkey-Chili Enchiladas
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Chopped cooked turkey or Chicken
1 can (4 oz) Chopped green Chiles, drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease a 13x9 baking dish or use smaller dishes if serving some now and freezing for later.
2. Mix turkey, 1 cup cheddar cheese, ricotta cheese, onions, green chiles, and coriander in a bowl.
3. Spoon about half a cup of the mixture onto the center of each tortilla.
4. Roll each tortilla around the filling, placing it seam side down in the dish.
5. In a nonreactive bowl, mix tomato sauce, salsa, chili powder, and cumin.
Alternatively, prepare two enchiladas per mini casserole pans lined with foil.
6. To serve immediately: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Pour the tomato sauce mixture over the tortillas. Cover with foil and bake for 15 minutes. Sprinkle with half a cup of cheddar cheese. Bake uncovered for an additional 10 minutes or until hot and bubbly.
7. To freeze: Pour the tomato mixture over the tortillas. Wrap the dish tightly with foil and freeze no longer than two months.
8. To serve from the freezer: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Place the covered dish in the oven for 45 minutes. Uncover, sprinkle with half a cup of cheddar cheese, and bake uncovered for an additional 15 to 20 minutes or until hot and bubbly.
